name: prolothar-queue-mining

metadata.name: prolothar-queue-mining
metadata.summary: algorithms for queue mining from event logs

documentation:
# Prolothar Queue Mining

Algorithms for queue mining (discovering discrete event simulations based on waiting queue models) from event logs

Based on the publication
> Boris Wiegand, Dietrich Klakow, and Jilles Vreeken.
> **Why Are We Waiting? Discovering Interpretable Models for Predicting Sojourn and Waiting Times.**
> In: *Proceedings of the SIAM International Conference on Data Mining (SDM), Minneapolis, MN.* 2023, pp. 352–360.

## Prerequisites

Python 3.11+

## Usage

If you want to run the algorithms on your own data, follow the steps below.

### Installing

```bash
pip install prolothar-queue-mining
```

### Creating a dataset

```python
from prolothar_queue_mining.model.job import Job
from prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue import CueMin

# our input data are jobs with an ID and their corresponding arrival resp. departure time
observed_arrivals = [
    (Job('A'), 3),
    (Job('B'), 4),
    (Job('C'), 5),
    (Job('D'), 6),
    (Job('E'), 7),
    (Job('F'), 8),
]
observed_departues = [
    (Job('A'), 4),
    (Job('B'), 7),
    (Job('C'), 11),
    (Job('D'), 12),
    (Job('E'), 13),
    (Job('F'), 14),
]

#you can add additional features to a job, example:
Job('4711', {'color': 'blue', size: 12})

cuemin = CueMin(verbose=True)

#if your jobs have features, which can have an influence on the service order or service time:
cuemin = CueMin(verbose=True, categorical_attribute_names = ['color'], numerical_attribute_names = ['size'])

#find and a print a waiting queue model
queue = cuemin.infer_queue(observed_arrivals, observed_departues)
print(queue)

#if you want to use domain knowledge to restrict the number of servers, e.g. min 2 and max 4:
cuemin = CueMin(search_strategy_name='linear-2-4')
queue = cuemin.infer_queue(observed_arrivals, observed_departues)
```

## Development

These instructions will get you a copy of the project up and running on your local machine for development and testing purposes.

### Additional Prerequisites
- make (optional)

### Compile Cython code

```bash
make cython
```

### Running the tests

```bash
make test
```

### Deployment

Optional requirement: Create a .pypirc file in the project root directory with your pypi authentication token:
```
[pypi]
username = __token__
password = pypi-AgEIcH...
```

1. Change the version in version.txt
2. Build the package

```bash
make clean_package
make package
```

3. Deploy the version to Pypi:
```bash
 make publish
 ```
or 
```bash
twine upload --skip-existing --verbose --config .pypirc dist/*
```

4. Create and push a tag for this version by

```bash
git tag -a $(cat version.txt) -m "describe this version"
git push --all && git push --tags
```

### Devcontainer

There is a decontainer definition in this project, which helps you to set up your environment.
At Stahl-Holding-Saar, we are behind a corporate proxy and cannot install dependencies from PyPi directly.
I yet have not found a stable solution to set the PIP_INDEX_URL and PIP_TRUSTED_HOST variables dynamically. 
In the current Dockerfile, I hardcoded the values, so you have to adapt them. 
If you know a solution to this problem, please contact me. 

## Versioning

We use [SemVer](http://semver.org/) for versioning.

## Authors

If you have any questions, feel free to ask one of our authors:

* **Boris Wiegand** - boris.wiegand@stahl-holding-saar.de

modules.name: setup
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import order is important!

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.fixed_sojourn_time_departure_time_predictor.FixedSojournTimeDepartureTimePredictor
documentation:
dummy predictor that predicts departure time for each job by adding
a constant sojourn time to the arrival time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.oracle_departure_time_predictor.OracleDepartureTimePredictor
documentation: dummy predictor that knows the true departure time for each job

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.load_cluster_predictor.LoadClusterDepartureTimePredictor
documentation:
a predictor that predicts sojourn time based on the number of jobs in the
system

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.sklearn_regression_predictor.SklearnRegressionDepartureTimePredictor

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.sklearn_regression_predictor.SklearnRegressionDepartureTimePredictor.__init__
documentation:
creates a new departure time predictor

Parameters
----------
regressor : RegressorMixin
    trained regression model that supports the sklearn API, i.e. it
    should offer a predict method. the predict method should return a
    prediction for the sojourn time of a job.
job_to_vector_transformer : JobToVectorTransformer
    used to transform the features of a job into a vector that can be understood
    by the regressor

departure time predictor that uses a simple sklearn regression. this
neglects the dependency between jobs and only considers the features of a
job.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.arrival_window_departure_time_predictor.ArrivalWindowDepartureTimePredictor

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.arrival_window_departure_time_predictor.ArrivalWindowDepartureTimePredictor.train_regression_model
documentation:
retrains the underlying regression model

Parameters
----------
arrivals : list[tuple[Job, int]]
    list of training arrival = jobs + corresponding arrival time
departures : list[tuple[Job, int]]
    list of training departures = jobs + corresponding departure time.
    used to compute training sojourn times, which is the target of
    the regression

a regressor that predicts departure time for each job by learning a regression
model with the attributes being the relative arrival times and job attributes
of a window around the job for which we want to predict the departure time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.ras_departure_time_predictor.RasDepartureTimePredictor
documentation:
predictor that uses an ArrivalProcessModel and a ServiceTimeModel from
prolothar_queue_mining.inference_queue.ras package

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.queue_departure_time_predictor.QueueDepartureTimePredictor
documentation: predictor that uses a queue model to predict departure times

documentation: predicts the departure time for each job in the arrivals list

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.departure_time_predictor.DepartureTimePredictor.predict_waiting_and_departure_times
documentation:
returns a 2-tuple. the first component is a dictionary with the predicted
waiting times per job. this can be None if this predictor does not support
prediction of waiting times. the second component is a dictionary with the
predicted departure times.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.prediction.departure_time.departure_time_predictor.DepartureTimePredictor.predict_waiting_and_departure_times_distribution
documentation:
predicts a list of possible waiting times and departure times for each job
given a fixed list of arrival times.

Returns
-------
tuple[dict[Job, list[int]], dict[Job, list[int]]]
    a list of predicted waiting times and a list of predicted departure times
    for each job
interface for departure time predictors

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.dummy.DummyNrOfServersEstimator
documentation: dummy estimator that outputs a fixed value

modules.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.sklearn.job_regression
documentation:
provides utility functions to train sklearn regressors based on the features
of Jobs

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.deltamax.Deltamax
documentation:
deltamax estimator for FCFS queues as described in

Andrew Keith and Darryl Ahner and Raymond Hill
"An order-based method for robust queue inference with stochastic arrival and departure times"
Computers & Industrial Engineering
2019

originally implemented in
https://github.com/ajkeith/UnobservableQueue.jl

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.utils.create_job_departure_order_list
documentation:
creates a list of arrival indices ordered by departure time.

Parameters
----------
observed_arrivals : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs and corresponding arrival times
observed_departures : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs and corresponding departure times

Returns
-------
list[int]
    a list of arrival indices ordered by departure time.
    the initial job list is [1,2,3,4,...,nr_of_jobs].
    this list is sorted by the departure time of each job.

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.utils.create_combined_job_index_list
documentation:
creates a list of job indices ordered by event time. the resulting list contains each
job index twice (arrival + departure). job indices increase with arrival time.

Parameters
----------
observed_arrivals : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs and corresponding arrival times
observed_departures : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs and corresponding departure times

Returns
-------
list[int]
    a list of job indices ordered by event time. the resulting list contains each
    job index twice (arrival + departure).

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.upper_bound_estimator.UpperBoundEstimator
documentation:
upper bound estimator for queues with a given queuing descipline. the upper
bound is given by the smallest number of servers for which all incoming jobs
can be served without waiting time. more servers would result in unused servers

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.nr_of_servers.lower_bound_estimator.LowerBoundEstimator
documentation:
lower bound estimator for queues with a given queuing descipline. the estimate
is given by the minimal number of servers to explain the departure order.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.record.Record
documentation: recording of quality score dependent on the input parameter space

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.mdl_service_time.MdlServiceTime

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.mdl_service_time.MdlServiceTime.__init__
documentation:
creates and intializes this OracleServiceTime instanz

Parameters
----------
environment : Environment
    used to get the current time
service_time : ServiceTime
    used to compute a fitness score
exit_time_per_job : dict[Job, float]
    used to get the exit time of a job. the service time is computed
    by the difference of exit time and current time

computes the service time from a already known exit time and computes
a fit of data score from an underlying distribution

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.cuemin.CueMin

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.cuemin.CueMin.__init__
documentation:
record_candidates

Parameters
----------
recording_enabled : bool, optional
    if True, then records score for search candidates for
    later analysis, by default True
record_candidates : bool, optional
    if True, then saves generated candidate models in a list for
    later analysis, by default False
seed_for_distributions : float, optional
    seed for created service time and batch size distributions during
    candidate generation, by default None
patience : int, optional
    accepted number of iterations with increasing number of servers
    without improvement, by default sys.maxsize
waiting_area_candidates : set[str], optional
    short names for waiting area candidates.
    can be FCFS,LCFS,LR,PQ-c,FLIFO,SIRO
    by default FCFS,LCFS,PQ-c,FLIFO

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.cuemin.CueMin.get_recording_dataframe
documentation:
returns a dataframe with the generated model candidates during search
and their corresponding MDL scores.

this dataframe is non-empty iff recording has not been deactivated in
the constructor. by default, recording is enabled.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.cuemin.CueMin.get_recorded_candidates
documentation:
the list of generated candidate models in the same order as
get_recording_dataframe(). this list is empty by default. recording
of candidates must be explicity enabled when calling the constructor.
by default, this is deactivated to save memory.
infers a queue using a MDL based selection criterion

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.search_strategy.search_strategy.SearchStrategy

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.search_strategy.search_strategy.SearchStrategy.search
documentation:
returns the best found queue together with its mdl score for the given
waiting area and observed data

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.search_strategy.search_strategy.SearchStrategy.get_recorded_candidates
documentation:
the list of generated candidate models in the same order as
get_recording_dataframe(). this list is empty by default. recording
of candidates must be explicity enabled when calling the constructor.
by default, this is deactivated to save memory.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.search_strategy.search_strategy.SearchStrategy.get_recording_dataframe
documentation:
returns a dataframe with the generated model candidates during search
and their corresponding MDL scores.

this dataframe is non-empty iff recording has not been deactivated in
the constructor. by default, recording is enabled.

determines how to search for the best number of servers for a given
waiting area.

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.mdl_score.compute_length_of_model
documentation: computes L(M)

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.mdl_score.compute_lower_bound_implied_by_model
documentation:
fast-to-compute lower bound for L(M) + L(D|M). it is computed by assuming
that all "nr_of_jobs" jobs are encoded with the shortest code implied by the model.

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.cuemin.mdl_score.compute_lower_bound_implied_by_model_and_data
documentation:
computes a lower bound for L(M) + L(D|M). this is slower than
"compute_lower_bound_implied_by_model" but also tighter

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.fcfs_c_one_throughput.FcfsCOneThroughput
documentation:
infers a queue with D=FCFS and c=1 and S=Degenerate(timesteps / number of jobs).
only with at least 1 job in the system are considered to filter-out phases
without the possibility to serve jobs.

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.utils.generate_distribution_candidates
documentation:
parameter inference for a pre-defined set of discrete distributions on the
given list of values

Parameters
----------
observations : list[float]
    list of observed values on which the distributions are supposed to be fitted
seed : float, optional
    seeds for the distributions, by default None

Yields
-------
Generator[DiscreteDistribution, None, None]
    yields MLE fitted distributions

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.queue_miner.QueueMiner

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.queue_miner.QueueMiner.infer_queue
documentation:
tries to infer structure and parameters of a Queue model from a list
of observed job arrivals and departues

Parameters
----------
observed_arrivals : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs together with the observed arrival time, ordered
    by arrival time (smaller arrival times at the beginning)
observed_departures : list[tuple[Job, float]]
    a list of jobs together with the observed departure time, ordered
    by departure time (smaller departure times at the beginning)

Returns
-------
Queue
    a Queue model inferred from observed_arrivals and observed_departues

interface for a queue miner, i.e. an algorithm that infers the structure
of a single queue from observations. in our case, only arrival and departure
times are given. the queue miner is supposed to infer the waiting area
with its discipline (e.g. LIFO) and the number of servers with the service
time distribution

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.batch.psm_batch_miner.PerformanceSpectrumBatchMiner
documentation: https://github.com/multi-dimensional-process-mining/psm-batchmining/blob/master/src/main/java/BatchMiner.java

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.batch.largest_gap_batch_miner.LargestGapBatchMiner
documentation:
jobs with the approximate the same departure time are assumed to be in a batch.
the departure time of the first and last job in a batch can differ up to a
auto-discovered max_delay parameter, the inter departure times are first sorted
and then max_delay refers to the largest inter departure times before the
largest gap

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.waiting_area.waiting_area_estimator.WaitingAreaEstimator

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.waiting_area.waiting_area_estimator.WaitingAreaEstimator.infer_waiting_area
documentation:
tries to infer structure and parameters of a WaitingArea model from a list
of observed job arrivals and departues

Parameters
----------
observed_arrivals : list[tuple[Job, int]]
    a list of jobs together with the observed arrival time, ordered
    by arrival time (smaller arrival times at the beginning)
observed_departures : list[tuple[Job, int]]
    a list of jobs together with the observed departure time, ordered
    by departure time (smaller departure times at the beginning)

Returns
-------
WaitingArea|None
    a waiting area model inferred from observed_arrivals and observed_departues.
    can be None if the estimator contains some criterion that denies fit
    on the given data.

interface of a waiting area estimator, i.e. an algorithm that infers the structure
of the waiting area of a queue from observations. in our case, only arrival and departure
times are given.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.inference.queue.keith_ahner_hill.KeithAhnerHill
documentation:
estimates the number of servers for FCFS and LCFS
queues, then takes the discipline with the least estimated number of servers
and estimates a mean service time

Andrew Keith and Darryl Ahner and Raymond Hill
"An order-based method for robust queue inference with stochastic arrival and departure times"
Computers & Industrial Engineering
2019

originally implemented in
https://github.com/ajkeith/UnobservableQueue.jl

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.sojourn_time.sojourn_time_observer.SojournTimeObserver

documentation:
method to notify that a job has exited

Parameters
----------
job : Job
    job that has exited the queue
arrival_time : int
    original time of arrival for this job
exit_time : int
    time when the job has left the queue

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.sojourn_time.sojourn_time_observer.SojournTimeObserver.copy
documentation: returns a deep copy of this sojourn time observer with the same state

interface of an observer for sojourn times, i.e. the time between arrival and
exit

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.sojourn_time.null_sojourn_time_observer.NullSojournTimeObserver
documentation: a sojourn time observer that does nothing (null object pattern)

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.waiting_time.waiting_time_observer.WaitingTimeObserver

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.waiting_time.waiting_time_observer.WaitingTimeObserver.notify
documentation:
method to notify that serving of a new job has been started

Parameters
----------
job : Job
    job for which serving has been started
arrival_time : float
    original time of arrival for this job
start_of_service_time : float
    current time at which processing has been start. the difference between
    start_of_service_time and arrival_time is the waiting_time.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.waiting_time.waiting_time_observer.WaitingTimeObserver.copy
documentation: returns a deep copy of this waiting time observer with the same state

interface of an observer for waiting times, i.e. the time between arrival and
start of service

documentation: a waiting time observer that does nothing (null object pattern)

documentation: returns the standard deviation of the observed sojourn time

documentation:

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.observer.sojourn_time.sojourn_time_recording_observer.SojournTimeRecordingObserver.get_timeseries_data
documentation:
returns a list of arrival times and corresponding sojourn times.
the list of arrival times can contain duplicates if two or more jobs arrived
at the same time.
an observer for (statistical) analysis of recorded sojourn times over time

Parameters

routing_table : dict[object, list[str]]

a router where a job follows a static list of stations

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.fixed_service_time.FixedServiceTime
documentation: a fixed, i.e. constant, service time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time_with_regressor.ServiceTimeWithRegressor
documentation:
a service time that uses a regression model and an error distribution
to make predictions

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time_with_offset.ServiceTimeWithOffset
documentation: a meta service time, that adds an offet to the prediction of its child

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.oracle_service_time_count_negative.OracleServiceTimeCountNegative

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.oracle_service_time_count_negative.OracleServiceTimeCountNegative.__init__
documentation:
creates and intializes this OracleServiceTimeCountNegative instance

Parameters
----------
environment : Environment
    used to get the current time
exit_time_per_job : dict[Job, float]
    used to get the exit time of a job. the service time is computed
    by the difference of exit time and current time

computes the service time from a already known exit time. counts how
often the service time would have been < 0, which is a hint for not enough
servers in the queue.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time_with_distribution.ServiceTimeWithDistribution
documentation:
a service time that follows any distribution. to get valid results,
returned service times are truncated to positive values and round to integer.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.oracle_service_time.OracleServiceTime

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.oracle_service_time.OracleServiceTime.__init__
documentation:
creates and intializes this OracleServiceTime instance

Parameters
----------
environment : Environment
    used to get the current time
exit_time_per_job : dict[Job, float]
    used to get the exit time of a job. the service time is computed
    by the difference of exit time and current time
computes the service time from a already known exit time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.load_dependent_service_time.LoadDependentServiceTime

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.load_dependent_service_time.LoadDependentServiceTime.__init__
documentation:
creates and intializes this LoadDependentServiceTime instance

Parameters
----------
sub_service_time_list: list[ServiceTime]
    service times used for increasing system load
load_threshold_list: list[int]
    thresholds that determine which sub service time model should be used
    depending on the number of jobs in the system.
    [1,3] means there are 3 sub models:
    - the first sub model is used if the system is empty,
    - the second sub model is used if at most 3 jobs are in the system
    - and the third sub model is used if there are more than 3 jobs in the system

depending on the system-load, i.e. the number of jobs in the system, a
different sub service time model is used

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.population.Population

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.population.Population.get_next_job
documentation: returns the next job if available or raises a StopIteration error

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.population.Population.copy
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this population with the same state.
if an attribute of this population uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.population.Population.set_seed
documentation:
sets the seed for any random generators used in this population. if
no randomness is included in this population, the action does not have
an effect
population of jobs (e.g. customers)

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.get_most_likely_service_time
documentation:

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.get_batch_service_time
documentation: determines the service (processing) time of the given batch of jobs

documentation:

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.set_seed
documentation: sets the seed for any random number generator used by this service time object

documentation:

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.get_mdl_of_model
documentation:
returns the encoded length of the model if supported. otherweise raises a

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.get_min_code_length_for_one_job
documentation:
returns the minimal code length to encode the service time of a job.
this can be used to compute a lower bound for L(D|M).
if not supported, this methoded raises a NotImplementedError

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.copy
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this service time with the same state.
if an attribute of this service time uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.service_time.service_time.ServiceTime.copy_mean
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this service time with the same state.
if an attribute of this service time uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.
 if this service time contains stochastic behavior, this will be
removed by setting it to the mean.
distribution or computation of the service time of jobs

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.list_population.ListPopulation
documentation:
a population that just consists of a static list. the jobs in this population
are yield in the order of the given list. if all jobs have been yielded, there
will no further jobs be available during simulation

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.infinite_population.InfinitePopulation
documentation:
mainly for testing purpose. a population that can generate (in theory) infinitely many
jobs with incrementing job ID and optionally also with randomly generated features

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.population.list_with_replacement_population.ListWithReplacementPopulation
documentation:
the jobs in this population are drawn from a finite list with replacement.
note that the job id will be overriden to ensure unique job ids.

documentation:
plots the PMF of the given distribution

Parameters
----------
distribution : DiscreteDistribution
    distribution for which the PMF is supposed to be plotted

show : bool, optional
    whether the plot should be shown, by default True
filepath : str, optional
    can be supplied to save the plot into a file, by default None

functions.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.distribution.plots.plot_pmfs
documentation:
plots the PMF of a list of distributions

Parameters
----------

show : bool, optional
    whether the plot should be shown, by default True
filepath : str, optional
    can be supplied to save the plot into a file, by default None
alpha : float, optional
    value between 0 and 1. controls transparency of the scatter plot markers.
    1 means no transparency. default is 0.3

two distributions are considered equal iff they are from the same type and

from different types behave exactly the same, they are considered different.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.distribution.discrete_distribution.DiscreteDistribution.fit_remove_outliers
documentation:
fits parameters with the following scheme:
fit() is used
then all observations with pmf <= ALLMOST_ZERO are removed
fit() is used again on remaining observations
template of a distribution, from which we can sample random numbers

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.distribution.two_sided_geometric.TwoSidedGeometricDistribution

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.distribution.two_sided_geometric.TwoSidedGeometricDistribution.__init__
documentation: creates a new distribution

a compound distribution with a geometric distribution for negative values and
a geometric distribution for positive values. a sample is drawn by first
drawing according to some pmf defined distribution over [+,0,-].

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.departure_scheduled.DepartureScheduledWaitingArea

documentation:
creates a new DepartureScheduledWaitingArea

----------

    assigns departure times to jobs. jobs are server in increasing order

    will never be served

waiting area where the jobs are popped according to their (scheduled)
departure time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
priority_feature_name : str
    name of the feature that contains the priority class for a job
priority_classes : list
    order of the priority classes. jobs with the first priority class
    in the list will be served first
sub_waiting_area_factory : Callable[[], WaitingArea]
    used to create the sub-waiting areas for each priority class.
    e.g. FirstComeFirstServeWaitingArea would enable FIFO serving for
    jobs with the same priority class

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea.add_job
documentation: adds a job to this waiting area

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea.has_next_job
documentation: returns True if there is a waiting job, otherwise returns False

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea.pop_next_job
documentation: returns the next waiting job if there is one. otherwise raises a StopIteration.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.priority_class.PriorityClassWaitingArea.pop_batch
documentation:
returns the next "batch_size" waiting jobs if there are enough jobs waiting.
otherwise raises a StopIteration.

Waiting area with priority classes. This waiting area consists of sub-waiting
area, one for each priority class. This enables FIFO or LIFO or any other handling of
jobs with the same priority

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.add_job
documentation: adds a job to this waiting area

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.has_next_job
documentation: returns True if there is a waiting job, otherwise returns False

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.pop_next_job
documentation:
returns the next waiting job if there is one. otherwise raises a StopIteration.
the order in which jobs are returned can be dependent on the nr_of_jobs_in_system,
i.e. defined as the number of jobs that have arrived but not exited the queue, yet.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.pop_batch
documentation:
returns the next "batch_size" waiting jobs if there are enough jobs waiting.
otherwise raises a StopIteration.
the order in which jobs are returned can be dependent on the nr_of_jobs_in_system,
i.e. defined as the number of jobs that have arrived but not exited the queue, yet.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.__len__

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.copy
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this waiting area with the same state.
if an attribute of this waiting area uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.copy_empty
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this waiting area. if the waiting area contains jobs,
the copy will contain no jobs. use copy() if you want to have the same
content.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.get_discipline_name
documentation:
returns the name of queue discipline, i.e. "D" in Kendal's notation,
e.g. FCFS, LCFS, PQ, SIRO, ...

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.any_order_iterator
documentation:
returns an iterator over the jobs in this waiting area. the jobs
are not necessarily ordered by their future departure order

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.get_mdl
documentation:
returns the minimum-description-length in bits to
encode this waiting area. this is used in CueMin to compute the model complexity.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.get_best_case_sort_key_for_synchronized_arrival
documentation:
returns a comparable key that orders jobs with equal arrival time such that the
number of required servers is minimized

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.waiting_area.WaitingArea.get_worst_case_sort_key_for_synchronized_arrival
documentation:
returns a comparable key that orders jobs with equal arrival time such that the
number of required servers is maximized
model for the waiting area of the queue. determines the discipline of the queue (e.g. FCFS)

is selected first

returns a deep copy of this waiting area with the same state.
if an attribute of this waiting area uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.

a priority waiting area where the priority (lower means first) is computed
by a regressor function

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.flifo.FlifoWaitingArea

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.flifo.FlifoWaitingArea.__init__
documentation:
creates a new FlifoWaitingArea

Parameters
----------
load_threshold : int
    up to this number of jobs in the system, jobs are popped in order
    of the low load mode. if the number of jobs in the system is higher
    than this number, jobs are popped in order of the high load mode.

fifo_on_low_load : bool, optional
    if True, then jobs are returned fifo if the number of jobs
    in the system is low and otherwise lifo.
    if False, jobs are returned lifo if the number of jobs
    in the system is low and otherwise fifo
    by default True

a waiting area that depending on the number of jobs in the system either serves
FIFO or LIFO (low and high load mode).

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.fcfs.FirstComeFirstServeWaitingArea
documentation: FIFO processing

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.random_order.RandomOrderWaitingArea
documentation: waiting area where jobs are served in completely random order

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.fast_lcfs.FastLastComeFirstServeWaitingArea
documentation:
faster implementation of LastComeFirstServeWaitingArea that does not look
at the time stamps but just uses a stack for its internal storage of jobs

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.batch_arrival_adapter.BatchArrivalAdapter
documentation:
translates any other arrival process into a batch arrival process. the arrival time
of each job in a batch is defined as the arrival time of the latest job in the
batch.

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.limited_arrival.LimitedArrival
documentation: limits the maximal number of arrived jobs by any other arrival process

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.null_arrival.NullArrival
documentation: a dummy arrival process, where no jobs arrive at all

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_with_distribution.ArrivalWithDistribution
documentation: "M" in Kendall's notation

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifier

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifier.should_job_a_be_served_before_job_b
documentation:
returns True iff job_a should be served before job_b

Parameters
----------
job_a : Job
job_b : Job
difference_in_arrival_time : int
    arrival time of job_a - arrival time of job_b
    => how much younger is job_a than job_b

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifier.train
documentation:
trains the classifier with a set of training relations

Parameters
----------
training_relations : TrainingRelations
    a list of relations, where each relation consists of two jobs,
    their difference in arrival time and a boolean flag whether job_a
    was served before job_b

interface of a pairwise priority classifier.
the classifier decides for two jobs which one is higher prioritized

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.SklearnPairwisePriorityClassifier
documentation:
interface of a pairwise priority classifier.
the classifier decides for two jobs which one is higher prioritized

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.add_job
documentation: adds a job to this waiting area

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.has_next_job
documentation: returns True if there is a waiting job, otherwise returns False

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.pop_next_job
documentation: returns the next waiting job if there is one. otherwise raises a StopIteration.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.pop_batch
documentation:
returns the next "batch_size" waiting jobs if there are enough jobs waiting.
otherwise raises a StopIteration.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.__len__
documentation: returns the number of jobs in this waiting area

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.copy
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this waiting area with the same state.
if an attribute of this waiting area uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.waiting_area.pairwise_priority_classifier.PairwisePriorityClassifierWaitingArea.get_discipline_name
documentation:
returns the name of queue discipline, i.e. "D" in Kendal's notation,
e.g. FCFS, LCFS, PQ, SIRO, ...

waiting area where a pairwise classifier decides on the order of the jobs.
the classifier decides for two jobs which one is higher prioritized

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_process.ArrivalProcess

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_process.ArrivalProcess.get_next_job
documentation:
returns the next job that arrives due to this arrival process together
with the time.
returns time, job

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_process.ArrivalProcess.copy
documentation:
returns a deep copy of this arrival process with the same state.
if an attribute of this arrival process uses a random seed and the
random seed is "None", this will result in different behavior during
simulation. this is not a bug, but the reason for this method.
otherwise use the "deepcopy" python module.

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_process.ArrivalProcess.set_seed
documentation: sets the seed used in random generators in this arrival process

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.arrival_process.ArrivalProcess.get_mean_arrival_rate
documentation:
returns the mean or expected arrival rate, i.e. the number of jobs
arriving at one timestep (usually a fractional).
Arrival process of the queue that determines which jobs arrive at which time

classes.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.recording_arrival.RecordingArrival

documentation: returns a list of recorded arrival times

methods.name: prolothar_queue_mining.model.arrival_process.recording_arrival.RecordingArrival.get_recorded_jobs

a meta arrival process, where the arrival times of jobs is recorded for
later reuse
